name: oznasi1-agent-flow
description: Grab a Jira task and spin up its workspace — a task pool in your sidebar that opens the right repos and pre-seeds a Claude Code agent.
author: Oz Nasi (oznasi1) — At-Bay

commands.title: Agent Flow: Sign in to Jira
Agent Flow: Sign out of Jira

agentFlow.jira.baseUrl.description: Base URL of your Atlassian Jira Cloud site (e.g. https://your-org.atlassian.net). Set during first-run setup.

agentFlow.jira.project.description: Jira project key to pull tasks from (e.g. ABC). Set during first-run setup.

agentFlow.reposRoot.description: Directory where your service repo checkouts live.

agentFlow.githubOrg.description: GitHub org used to clone repos that aren't checked out yet (reserved — not yet used).

agentFlow.repoBlocklist.description: Directory names under reposRoot to exclude from task-target discovery (infra, tooling, generated repos). Hidden dirs are always skipped.

enumDescriptions: Unassigned tasks in the active sprint
Your tasks in the active sprint
All tasks assigned to you
All tasks in the active sprint
Tasks not in the active sprint
agentFlow.defaultFilter.description: Default task filter lens shown when Agent Flow opens.

agentFlow.filters.size.description: Show the Size lens (Any/S/M/L) in the task-pool sidebar. Turn off to hide it. Applies on refresh/reload.

agentFlow.filters.status.description: Show the Status chip row in the task-pool sidebar. Turn off to hide it. Applies on refresh/reload.

agentFlow.filters.repo.description: Show the repo multiselect filter in the task-pool sidebar. Turn off to hide it. Applies on refresh/reload.

agentFlow.filters.search.description: Show the fuzzy title search box in the task-pool sidebar. Turn off to hide it. Applies on refresh/reload.

agentFlow.seedAgent.description: After opening a workspace, pre-fill the Claude Code panel with the task plan (you press Enter to start).

enumDescriptions: 1 repo → open that repo; multiple → one multi-root workspace
Always one multi-root workspace
Always one window per repo
Ask each time (when more than one repo)
agentFlow.workspaceMode.description: How to open the repos a task touches.

enumDescriptions: Ask each time you take a task
Always open a task in a new window
Always open a task in the current window (replaces what's here)
Always pick an existing .code-workspace to open the task into
agentFlow.openIn.description: Where to open a task you take: a new window, or reuse the current one.

agentFlow.taskMode.markdownDescription: Which prompt mode to seed when taking a task: `ask` to choose each time, or the `id` of one of `#agentFlow.promptModes#`.

agentFlow.promptModes.markdownDescription: Prompt modes offered when taking a task. Each has an `id`, `label`, and `prompt` template. Placeholders: `{key}`, `{summary}`, `{url}`, `{brief}`, `{files}`. Add your own (e.g. a `debug` mode).

default.label: Plan first
default.prompt: Jira {key}: "{summary}". Read the task brief at {brief} for context and the repos involved. Propose a step-by-step PLAN for this task and wait for my approval — do not edit any code yet. Ticket: {url}{files}

default.label: Implementation
default.prompt: Jira {key}: "{summary}". Read the task brief at {brief} for context and the repos involved. Begin implementing. Confirm your approach with me only if something is ambiguous. Ticket: {url}{files}

agentFlow.explorePrompt.markdownDeprecationMessage: Deprecated — use the per-action prompts under `agentFlow.explorePrompts`. If you customized this, its value is migrated into the **Enhance knowledge / flow** action automatically.
agentFlow.explorePrompt.markdownDescription: Prompt seeded when you start an **Explore** session (no ticket). Placeholders: `{summary}` (your focus), `{brief}`, `{files}`.
agentFlow.explorePrompt.default: Exploration session — no Jira ticket yet. Focus: "{summary}". A brief listing the repos in scope is at {brief}. Help me understand how this works: map the relevant code paths, explain the flow, and flag anything surprising or worth a follow-up ticket. Don't change code unless I ask.{files}

enumDescriptions: Choose an action each time you click Explore
Open a Jira ticket — explore, then create a ticket capturing the findings
Enhance knowledge / flow — map the code paths and explain how it works
Debug — reproduce and root-cause a problem
General — open-ended working session
agentFlow.exploreMode.markdownDescription: Which Explore action to start. `ask` shows a picker each time; otherwise that action's prompt is seeded directly. Prompts are editable under `agentFlow.explorePrompts`.

agentFlow.explorePrompts.jiraTicket.markdownDescription: Prompt seeded for the **Open a Jira ticket** Explore action. Placeholders: `{summary}` (your focus), `{brief}`, `{files}`.
agentFlow.explorePrompts.jiraTicket.default: Exploration session. Focus: "{summary}". A brief listing the repos in scope is at {brief}. Dig into this, then draft and create a Jira ticket that captures what you found — a clear problem statement, the affected code paths, and a proposed approach. Add the `claude-code` label to the ticket, and share the ticket key and URL.{files}

agentFlow.explorePrompts.knowledge.markdownDescription: Prompt seeded for the **Enhance knowledge / flow** Explore action. Placeholders: `{summary}` (your focus), `{brief}`, `{files}`.
agentFlow.explorePrompts.knowledge.default: Exploration session — no Jira ticket yet. Focus: "{summary}". A brief listing the repos in scope is at {brief}. Help me understand how this works: map the relevant code paths, explain the flow, and flag anything surprising or worth a follow-up ticket. Don't change code unless I ask.{files}

agentFlow.explorePrompts.debug.markdownDescription: Prompt seeded for the **Debug** Explore action. Placeholders: `{summary}` (your focus), `{brief}`, `{files}`.
agentFlow.explorePrompts.debug.default: Debugging session — no Jira ticket yet. Focus: "{summary}". A brief listing the repos in scope is at {brief}. Reproduce the problem, trace it to a root cause, and explain what's going wrong with evidence from the code. Propose a fix, but don't change code unless I ask.{files}

agentFlow.explorePrompts.general.markdownDescription: Prompt seeded for the **General** Explore action. Placeholders: `{summary}` (your focus), `{brief}`, `{files}`.
agentFlow.explorePrompts.general.default: Working session. Focus: "{summary}". A brief listing the repos in scope is at {brief}. Help me make progress on this — ask what I need if it's unclear before diving in. Don't change code unless I ask.{files}

agentFlow.exploreSlackDm.markdownDescription: Per-action: when checked, the seeded prompt asks the agent to send you a Slack DM summarizing the session when it ends. Off by default. (The agent does this via its own Slack connector.)

agentFlow.prReviewStatus.description: Task status that reveals the "Address PR" button on a card (matched case-insensitively against the Jira status name). When a task reaches this status, clicking the button kicks off an agent — in a worktree — that finds the task's GitHub PR and assesses whether it's ready for our fixes.

agentFlow.prReviewAutoFix.description: After the PR-review agent assesses the PR, let it proceed to implement the requested changes on the PR's branch. Turn off to have it only assess and report back.

agentFlow.prReviewPrompt.markdownDescription: Prompt seeded for the **Address PR** kick-off. The agent locates the task's GitHub PR by its Jira key and assesses readiness; when `#agentFlow.prReviewAutoFix#` is on, a fixing instruction is appended automatically. Placeholders: `{key}`, `{summary}`, `{url}`, `{brief}`, `{files}`.
agentFlow.prReviewPrompt.default: Jira {key} ({url}): "{summary}". This task has an open GitHub PR — all our PRs carry the Jira key in their title and branch. Using `gh` (or the GitHub tools available to you): find the PR for {key}, run `gh pr checkout` to bring its branch into this worktree, then assess whether it's ready for us to work on — unresolved review comments and requested changes, CI status, merge conflicts, and approval state. Summarize what you find.{files}

enumDescriptions: Ask each time
Always create a per-task git worktree/branch
Always work in the repo directly
agentFlow.worktree.description: Whether to isolate the task in a git worktree. Worktrees are created inside each repo at .claude/worktrees/<KEY> (git-excluded automatically).

agentFlow.batchLaunchConfirmThreshold.markdownDescription: When you multi-select tasks (with the repo filter narrowed to a single repo) and launch them in parallel, batches **larger than** this number prompt a confirmation first — a guard against accidentally opening a swarm of windows, one per task.

agentFlow.trackOpenWindows.description: Track your open Agent Flow windows so a task can be opened into a window you already have open (shown as extra choices in the "Open the task where?" picker).

agentFlow.stampLabelOnWrite.description: Add the provenance label (see agentFlow.provenanceLabel) whenever this extension updates a Jira item (e.g. a status change), to mark automated changes.

agentFlow.provenanceLabel.description: Label stamped on Jira items this extension updates when agentFlow.stampLabelOnWrite is enabled.

agentFlow.marketplaces.markdownDescription: GitHub repos that are Claude Code plugin marketplaces. Accepts `owner/repo` or a full github.com URL. Managed from the Marketplace panel (the puzzle-piece button beside the Deck).
